sanskrit:
कामक्रोधविमुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम्।
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम्॥
transliteration: kāma-krodha-vimuktānāṁ yatīnāṁ yata-cetasām | abhito brahma-nirvāṇaṁ vartate viditātmanām ||
literal_meaning: For those freed from desire and anger, disciplined sages whose minds are controlled, liberation in Brahman is imminent for the self-realized.
contextual_meaning: When the nervous system is no longer hijacked by craving or aversion, the prefrontal “witness” (viditātman) stabilizes; mokṣa is no longer a future goal but a present neuro-spiritual state.
psychological_state: High vagal-tone, low amygdala reactivity; the mind rests in open monitoring without being pulled toward or away from objects.
trigger_scenario: You’re stuck in traffic, late for a meeting; instead of honking or checking your phone, you notice the heat of irritation rise and dissolve—freedom is measured in that 30-second gap.
anchor_text: Drop craving and anger; the calm mind already tastes freedom.
